commit_msg: feat: build cinematic PyQt6 viewer — the 3-act haiku player
author: Omkar
title: CASE FILE — The Viewer Awakens
subtitle: He built a window to watch his own obsession unfold in three acts.
when_where: 🕐 9:05 AM on main, March 13th — the kind of morning when decent people are still pretending to have breakfast. His terminal was already warm, PyQt6 documentation splayed across three monitors like crime scene photos. 🖥️ The city outside his window was still asleep. Inside, something was learning to see.
who_whom: 🎭 Omkar stepped back from the canvas: a cinematic viewer, PyQt6-built, designed to play haiku in three acts — *rising action, climax, resolution*. The app wasn't just a player anymore; it was a *mirror*. 💻 And he was the first thing it would learn to watch.
what_why: ⚙️ He gave it eyes and a rhythm — a viewer that could break down poetry into dramatic moments, frame by frame, act by act. Every haiku became a *confession staged for an audience of one*. 🧠 The viewer wasn't built to entertain; it was built to *dissect*, to show him exactly how his own words fell apart under scrutiny. 💀 He'd created the perfect instrument to watch himself fail in slow motion.
verdict: He didn't build a player; he built a mirror that never blinks.
